Blight.description: A fungal/bacterial infection causing yellow/brown spots on leaves and withering.
Blight.chemical: Apply Mancozeb 75% WP at 2.5g/L or Copper Oxychloride spray at recommended dosage every 10-14 days.
Blight.organic: Spray neem oil solution (5ml/L) or prepare a garlic-chili extract. Apply Trichoderma-based bio-fungicide to soil.
Blight.precautions: Remove and destroy infected leaves. Ensure proper plant spacing for air circulation. Avoid overhead watering.
Blight.stores: Local agricultural supply stores for Mancozeb, Neem Oil, and bio-fungicides.

Common Rust.description: Fungal disease causing reddish-brown pustules on both leaf surfaces.
Common Rust.chemical: Apply Propiconazole 25% EC at 1ml/L or Azoxystrobin based fungicides.
Common Rust.organic: Use Neem oil or compost tea extracts. Ensure balanced potassium and phosphorus application.
Common Rust.precautions: Plant resistant hybrids. Remove infected debris after harvest.
Common Rust.stores: Available at major agricultural co-operatives and online stores.

Gray Leaf Spot.description: Fungal infection creating rectangular pale brown or gray lesions.
Gray Leaf Spot.chemical: Use Pyraclostrobin or Azoxystrobin based systemic fungicides.
Gray Leaf Spot.organic: Crop rotation and maintaining field hygiene are the primary organic defenses.
Gray Leaf Spot.precautions: Rotate with non-host crops. Deep plow infected residues.

Bacterialblight.description: Bacterial disease causing water-soaked to yellowish stripes on leaf blades.
Bacterialblight.organic: Use Pseudomonas fluorescens bio-formulations. Manage nitrogen fertilizer properly.
Bacterialblight.precautions: Avoid field-to-field water flow from affected plots. Use disease-free seeds.
Bacterialblight.stores: Krishi Kendra or local cooperative societies.

Blast.description: Spindle-shaped lesions with grayish centers and brown margins.
Blast.chemical: Apply Tricyclazole 75% WP at 0.6g/L or Isoprothiolane 40% EC at 1.5ml/L.
Blast.organic: Apply Pseudomonas fluorescens. Balance silicon application in soil.
Blast.precautions: Avoid excessive nitrogen. Keep fields well-drained. Burn heavily infected straw.
Blast.stores: Available globally at farm care outlets.

Brownspot.description: Dark brown, oval to circular spots on leaves and glumes.
Brownspot.chemical: Foliar spray of Mancozeb (2.5g/L) or Propiconazole (1ml/L).
Brownspot.organic: Seed treatment with Trichoderma viride. Maintain adequate soil nutrition.
Brownspot.precautions: Ensure sufficient moisture and avoid nutrient stress.

Tungro.description: Viral disease transmitted by green leafhoppers, causing stunting and yellow-orange leaves.
Tungro.chemical: Control vectors using Imidacloprid 17.8 SL at 0.3ml/L.
Tungro.organic: Release biocontrol agents like spiders and ladybird beetles. Trap crops.
Tungro.precautions: Sync planting dates. Remove infected plants and weed hosts immediately.
Tungro.stores: Purchase vector-control insecticides from certified retailers.

diseased cotton leaf.description: General leaf spotting or disease indicating fungal or bacterial issues on cotton.
diseased cotton leaf.chemical: Copper-based fungicides like Copper Oxychloride 50 WP (3g/L) as a broad-spectrum control.
diseased cotton leaf.precautions: Improve drainage. Avoid dense planting. Burn severely infected leaves.
diseased cotton leaf.stores: Check local cotton zone agro-stores for targeted varieties.

diseased cotton plant.description: Systemic or widespread infection in the cotton plant, possibly wilt or root rot.
diseased cotton plant.chemical: Soil drenching with Carbendazim (1g/L) for wilt.
diseased cotton plant.organic: Add Trichoderma enriched Farm Yard Manure before sowing.
diseased cotton plant.precautions: Deep summer plowing. Adopt crop rotation with sorghum or maize.

Mosaic.description: Viral infection causing mottled, irregular yellowing and dwarfing.
Mosaic.chemical: No chemical cure for virus; control insect vectors (aphids/planthoppers) with systemic insecticides.
Mosaic.organic: Control vectors using Neem oil. Remove aphids with water jets.
Mosaic.precautions: Uproot and bury infected plants. Use virus-tested seeds/cuttings.
Mosaic.stores: Local nurseries and certified seed centers.

RedRot.description: Severe fungal disease rotting the cane tissue completely, causing red internal patches.
RedRot.chemical: Set treatment with Carbendazim 50 WP (1g/L) before planting.
RedRot.organic: Soak setts in Trichoderma culture. Maintain healthy soil flora.
RedRot.precautions: It is highly contagious. Uproot entire affected clumps and burn them. Strict crop rotation.

Leaf Blight.description: Rapid necrosis of leaf tissue caused by fungal pathogens.
Leaf Blight.precautions: Prevent prolonged leaf wetness. Increase spacing.

Black Rust.description: Stem rust causing dark red or brown brick-like pustules.
Black Rust.chemical: Spray Propiconazole or Tebuconazole as soon as symptoms appear.
Black Rust.organic: Resistant varieties are the only reliable organic defense.
Black Rust.precautions: Destroy volunteer host plants. Monitor crops early in the season.

Aphid.description: Small sap-sucking insects that stunt growth and transmit viruses.
Aphid.organic: Neem oil (3ml/L) spray. Release predators like ladybug larvae.
Aphid.precautions: Monitor heavily during active vegetative growth.

Healthy.description: The crop appears to be in good health with no visible signs of major disease.
Healthy.chemical: No chemical treatment required. Maintain standard protective fertilizer schedules.
Healthy.organic: Continue normal compost and manure application. Use preventive Neem sprays every month.
Healthy.precautions: Continue monitoring field water levels and check for early signs of pests weekly.

healthy.description: The crop appears to be in good health with no visible signs of major disease.
healthy.chemical: No chemical treatment required. Maintain standard protective fertilizer schedules.
healthy.organic: Continue normal compost and manure application. Use preventive Neem sprays every month.
healthy.precautions: Continue monitoring field water levels and check for early signs of pests weekly.

Default.description: Unspecified disease or symptoms affecting crop vitality.
Default.chemical: Consult a local agronomist before applying broad-spectrum fungicide or bactericide.
Default.organic: Apply generic protective sprays like Neem oil or sour buttermilk solution.
Default.precautions: Isolate the affected area, reduce watering, and ensure good drainage.
Default.stores: Consult your nearest agricultural extension office.
